rulings.Case Title: YIN FALK VS. LINDA S CATRON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Judgment Creditor Yin Falk'S Motion For Order Compelling Responses By Judgment Creditor Richard Warren To Post-Judgment Discovery Requests
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 22, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF YIN FALK's Motion For Order Compelling Responses By Judgment Creditor Richard Warren To Post-Judgment Discovery Requests. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, with monetary sanctions awarded against defendant/judgment creditor Richard Warren in the amount of $1,440 (assuming no hearing). The Motion is granted on the grounds set forth in the moving papers, and: (1) the Motion is not opposed; (2) Mr. Warren has not submitted any response or proposed response to the Post-Judgment Interrogatories or Demand for Production of Documents. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KATHLEEN BLOCK VS. RETIREMENT OF THE BOARD OF THE CITY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Writ Of Mandamus; Notice Of Lodging
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 22, 2012, Line 4. PETITIONER KATHLEEN BLOCK Motion For Writ Of Mandamus. Denied. Applying the independent judgment standard, the weight of the evidence shows that Officer Block's back injuries and pain did not have a real and measurable connection to his death. Petitioner did not meet her burden of showing that the administrative findings were contrary to the weight of the evidence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. RMF ENGINEERING, INC., A MARYLAND CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 22, 2012, Line 11. DEFENDANT PERMA-PIPE, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the first cause of action and sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the third and fourth causes of action. The negligence claim is adequately pled. Plaintiff is granted leave to allege, if it can do so in good faith, that the alleged express warranty/statement was part of an agreement between it and Perma-Pipe and that it was either in privity of contract or had direct dealings with Perma-Pipe. The request for judicial notice is denied because the document sought to be judicially noticed is not one that may be judicially noticed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROY MILLER et al VS. MARCUS DANIEL MERCHASIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 22, 2012, Line 12. DEFENDANT WILLIAM HEDDEN, MARCUS MERCHASIN, CONSOLIDATED ADJUSTING, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Granted in its entirety. The commercial speech exception is inapplicable. Defendants satisfied their burden of showing that all of plaintiffs' claims are based on activity protected by the anti-SLAPP statute and plaintiffs have not satisfied their burden of showing that any of their claims have the requisite minimal merit to preclude dismissal. Plaintiffs' first claim lacks merit because defendants had reasonable grounds to file the prior lawsuit. Plaintiffs' remaining claims are barred by the statute of limitations and/or the litigation privilege. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DARRYL LEUNG VS. TONY HSAIO TANG TU, DOING BUSINESS AS H.T. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 22, 2012, Line 14. DEFENDANT TONY HSAIO TANG TU, DOING BUSINESS AS H.T. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to the third/fourth and sixth causes of action and overruled as to the seventh, eighth, and ninth causes of action. Plaintiff has leave to amend the third/fourth cause of action to correct the typo that lists the first page of the cause of action as the third cause of action and the second page as the fourth cause of action and to specifically allege what fraudulent statements Defendant made, when they were made, who they were made to, and how Plaintiff relied on the statements. Plaintiff has leave to amend the sixth cause of action to correct the violation of the thirty day notice requirement pursuant to Civil Code section 1782. The seventh, eighth and ninth causes of action are either adequately pled and/or the demurrer to them serves no useful purpose in advancing this litigation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DARRYL LEUNG VS. TONY HSAIO TANG TU, DOING BUSINESS AS H.T. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 22, 2012, Line 14. DEFENDANT TONY HSAIO TANG TU, DOING BUSINESS AS H.T. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Granted in part. Page sixteen (the exemplary damages attachment), the words "Immaculate Construction" in paragraph five on page twenty-four, paragraph six on page twenty-four, and paragraph nine on page twenty-five are stricken. All other portions of the complaint sought to be stricken are adequately pled and thus the motion to strike those portions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
